title: Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once
candidate_claim: The paper introduces Benchmark Agent as a fully autonomous agentic system for benchmark building.

title: Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once
candidate_claim: Benchmark Agent's generated benchmarks are broadly validated as high quality across benchmark types.
reference_rewrite: In the reported main evaluation, generated benchmarks achieved about 96-98% human acceptance and UIA scores from 68.54 to 81.48.

title: Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once
candidate_claim: The generated benchmarks show consistent scaling trends for all evaluated model families across every benchmark.
reference_rewrite: The generated benchmarks are reported to show consistent Qwen3.5 scaling trends on three applicable benchmarks from 2B to 27B.

title: Benchmark Everything Everywhere All at Once
candidate_claim: Rapid benchmark refresh for emerging models, new domains, and changing user needs is a design context for Benchmark Agent.

title: TeleSWEBench: A Commit-Driven Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-Powered Software Engineering in Telecommunications
candidate_claim: TeleSWEBench consists of 734 questions with executable unit tests.

title: TeleSWEBench: A Commit-Driven Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-Powered Software Engineering in Telecommunications
candidate_claim: TeleSWEBench establishes a 25% success-rate ceiling for autonomous software engineering frameworks in telecom.
reference_rewrite: On TeleSWEBench, the best-performing evaluated ASE tools reached up to a 25% success rate for producing shippable code.

title: TeleSWEBench: A Commit-Driven Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-Powered Software Engineering in Telecommunications
candidate_claim: TeleSWEBench evaluates only final functional correctness and does not separately test localization ability.
reference_rewrite: TeleSWEBench uses a two-stage evaluation pipeline designed to decouple file-localization or architectural navigation skills from functional code-generation correctness.

title: TeleSWEBench: A Commit-Driven Benchmark for Evaluating LLM-Powered Software Engineering in Telecommunications
candidate_claim: TeleSWEBench proves telecom software agents are ready for production deployment.

title: SenseJudge: Human-Centric Preference-Driven Judgment Framework
candidate_claim: SenseJudge is reported to score 70.66 overall on LLM-as-a-Personalized-Judge and outperform compared baselines by 10.68 percentage points on average.

title: SenseJudge: Human-Centric Preference-Driven Judgment Framework
candidate_claim: SenseJudge matches or surpasses reward models on RewardBench without training.
reference_rewrite: On RewardBench, SenseJudge outperformed compared general LLM-as-a-judge methods and was 1.65% behind the generative reward model without training.

title: SenseJudge: Human-Centric Preference-Driven Judgment Framework
candidate_claim: SenseBench was built from single-turn synthetic prompts rather than real-world multi-turn interactions.
reference_rewrite: The paper proposes SenseBench as a diverse instruction-following benchmark derived from real-world multi-turn interactions.

title: SenseJudge: Human-Centric Preference-Driven Judgment Framework
candidate_claim: SenseBench's coverage of eight common topics gives background context for interpreting SenseJudge evaluations.

title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
candidate_claim: The paper claims MLLM judges tend to favor plausible text over perceptually correct answers when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues.

title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
candidate_claim: The proposed method delivers double-digit improvements over Qwen3-VL-4B-Thinking in both pairwise and batch-level evaluations.
reference_rewrite: The proposed method is reported to improve over Qwen3-VL-4B-Thinking by 4% in pairwise evaluation and 11% in batch-level evaluation.

title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
candidate_claim: The ablation shows MMPR-v1.2 is the better training data than PPJD across all measured metrics.

title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
candidate_claim: Table 1 proves visual-language judges understand human preferences better than human annotators.

title: TukaBench: A Culturally Grounded Jailbreak Benchmark for African Languages
candidate_claim: TukaBench contains 986 prompts per African language across seven African languages, with African-language prompts machine-translated and human post-edited.

title: TukaBench: A Culturally Grounded Jailbreak Benchmark for African Languages
candidate_claim: Across evaluated models, African-language prompting produced the same sharp rise in deflection for every model.
reference_rewrite: Across evaluated models, the more consistent pattern was a sharp rise in deflection, where responses were neither harmful completions nor explicit refusals.

title: TukaBench: A Culturally Grounded Jailbreak Benchmark for African Languages
candidate_claim: TukaBench is only a direct translation of JailbreakBench into seven African languages.
reference_rewrite: The paper introduces TukaBench as a jailbreak benchmark for seven African languages that extends JailbreakBench beyond direct translation through four settings.

title: TukaBench: A Culturally Grounded Jailbreak Benchmark for African Languages
candidate_claim: Judge-human agreement concerns are relevant background for interpreting TukaBench results in lower-resource and non-Latin-script languages.

title: Beyond Ideal Instruction: A Comprehensive Framework for Evaluating LLMs in Realistic Interactions
candidate_claim: RUT-Bench consists of 1,638 test samples across 59 executable tool-use environments in multiple domains.

title: Beyond Ideal Instruction: A Comprehensive Framework for Evaluating LLMs in Realistic Interactions
candidate_claim: RUT-Bench shows all current LLMs fail below 40% overall success in real-world tool use.
reference_rewrite: In RUT-Bench experiments, no tested LLM exceeded 40% overall success, and nearly all dropped under more complicated non-ideal user inputs.

title: Beyond Ideal Instruction: A Comprehensive Framework for Evaluating LLMs in Realistic Interactions
candidate_claim: RUT-Bench directly measures live external API latency, rate limits, and backend changes.
reference_rewrite: RUT-Bench uses deterministic Python class environments that abstract away the operational friction of real-world external APIs.

title: Beyond Ideal Instruction: A Comprehensive Framework for Evaluating LLMs in Realistic Interactions
candidate_claim: RUT-Bench proves tool-calling LLMs can autonomously complete real users' tasks without human oversight.

title: SemRepo: A Knowledge Graph for Research Software and Its Scholarly Ecosystem
candidate_claim: SemRepo is an RDF knowledge graph with over 81 million triples describing nearly 200,000 GitHub repositories associated with scientific research.

title: SemRepo: A Knowledge Graph for Research Software and Its Scholarly Ecosystem
candidate_claim: SemRepo's construction pipeline fully enumerates all GitHub repositories associated with scientific research.

title: SemRepo: A Knowledge Graph for Research Software and Its Scholarly Ecosystem
section_locator: Section 3.3 Linkage to External Scholarly Knowledge Graphs
candidate_claim: SemRepo links every contributor in the graph to a SemOpenAlex author profile.
reference_rewrite: SemRepo links 11,867 contributors, approximately 6% of all persons in SemRepo, enabling joint analysis of software development activity and bibliometric author profiles.

title: SemRepo: A Knowledge Graph for Research Software and Its Scholarly Ecosystem
candidate_claim: SemRepo should be treated as a mandatory audit standard for conference reviewers assessing research software.

title: Journal Research Data Policies in Materials Science
candidate_claim: The study surveys research data policies for 171 materials science journals across 17 publishers using an expanded data-and-code coding framework.

title: Journal Research Data Policies in Materials Science
candidate_claim: Nearly all materials science journals have research data policies and data availability statements.
reference_rewrite: Among the surveyed materials science journals, 98% had a research data policy and 95% mentioned a data availability statement.

title: Journal Research Data Policies in Materials Science
candidate_claim: Code sharing is required by 70% of the surveyed materials science journals.
reference_rewrite: In the surveyed journals, code sharing was encouraged but optional for 70% and required for only 1.8%.

title: Journal Research Data Policies in Materials Science
candidate_claim: Open and reproducible materials-science research depends on the availability of data, code, and common metadata standards.

title: Towards a Metadata Schema for Energy Research Software
candidate_claim: The paper develops an energy research software metadata schema from requirement analysis and evaluates it through user testing.

title: Towards a Metadata Schema for Energy Research Software
candidate_claim: User testing showed that ERSmeta was straightforward for participants and had no meaningful usability drawbacks.
reference_rewrite: Participants found ERSmeta's elements useful and relevant, but identified the high number of elements and system complexity as significant drawbacks.

title: Towards a Metadata Schema for Energy Research Software
candidate_claim: ERSmeta consists of ten top-level elements clustered into 86 thematic areas.
reference_rewrite: ERSmeta consists of 86 top-level elements clustered into ten thematic areas.

title: Towards a Metadata Schema for Energy Research Software
candidate_claim: Domain-specific metadata schemas motivate work on improving findability, reusability, and FAIR4RS alignment for research software.

candidate_claim: The paper frames AI model selection as an MCDM problem and proposes an evidence-based framework combining automated data collection, a knowledge graph, and MCDM principles.

title: Evidence-Driven Decision Support for AI Model Selection in Research Software Engineering
candidate_claim: The open Mendeley Data repository independently guarantees that every result in the paper is reproducible and extensible.

title: Evidence-Driven Decision Support for AI Model Selection in Research Software Engineering
candidate_claim: The GitHub Repository Extraction pipeline selected 37,176 repositories and crawled 4,610.

title: Evidence-Driven Decision Support for AI Model Selection in Research Software Engineering
candidate_claim: The current ModelSelect implementation should be applied unchanged across all research-software language ecosystems.

title: OpenDORS: A dataset of openly referenced open research software
candidate_claim: OpenDORS presents 134,352 unique open research software projects and 134,154 source code repositories referenced in open-access literature.

title: OpenDORS: A dataset of openly referenced open research software
candidate_claim: OpenDORS v2025-11 is a complete census of all open research software projects across the scholarly record.
reference_rewrite: OpenDORS v2025-11 contains 134,352 research software projects based on repository URLs mined from 2,656,582 PMC, ArXiv, and JOSS publications.

title: OpenDORS: A dataset of openly referenced open research software
candidate_claim: The authors release only the OpenDORS dataset, without releasing the construction workflow or implementation package.
reference_rewrite: The authors release the OpenDORS dataset and construction workflow under an open license, and also release the open-source Python package opendors2 implementing the construction logic.

title: OpenDORS: A dataset of openly referenced open research software
candidate_claim: OpenDORS can be used to benchmark closed-source and unshared research software projects.

candidate_claim: The Software Observatory is an OpenEBench web portal that consolidates software metadata from multiple sources for research software analysis.

section_locator: Section 2.1 Metadata extraction, normalisation, and enrichment
candidate_claim: The Software Observatory permanently ingests metadata from all research software registries across all domains.
reference_rewrite: The Software Observatory initially ingests metadata from registries such as bio.tools, Bioconda, Bioconductor, Galaxy ToolShed, bioinformatics-tagged SourceForge, and Galaxy Europe.

title: The Software Observatory: aggregating and analysing software metadata for trend computation and FAIR assessment
candidate_claim: The Software Observatory initially collected exactly 65,942 software metadata records from all scientific registries.
reference_rewrite: The Software Observatory initially collected over 65,942 metadata records from major Life Sciences registries, including bio.tools, Bioconda, and the Galaxy ToolShed.

title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models
candidate_claim: ORCE improves calibration and failure prediction on reasoning and knowledge-intensive benchmarks while largely preserving answer accuracy.

title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models
candidate_claim: ORCE learns true correctness probabilities for verbalized confidence without using any model-internal information.
reference_rewrite: ORCE uses a sampling-based surrogate of correctness likelihood to supervise verbalized confidence without relying on internal model probabilities.

title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models
section_locator: Appendix A.4, Limitations of the idealized analysis
candidate_claim: ORCE directly maximizes expected Spearman-correlation reward during DPO training.
reference_rewrite: ORCE's practical algorithm uses SC to construct preference pairs, and DPO performs a smoothed preference-optimization step rather than directly maximizing expected SC reward.

title: ORCE: Order-Aware Alignment of Verbalized Confidence in Large Language Models
candidate_claim: ORCE adopts DPO to align the confidence LLM with its surrogate using the SC reward.

title: Unsupervised Confidence Calibration for Reasoning LLMs from a Single Generation
candidate_claim: The paper proposes unsupervised confidence calibration for reasoning LLMs under a single-generation inference constraint.

title: Unsupervised Confidence Calibration for Reasoning LLMs from a Single Generation
candidate_claim: The method removes repeated generations from both training and inference by never using self-consistency sampling.
reference_rewrite: The method uses self-consistency offline on unlabeled data to construct supervision for a single-pass confidence predictor, reducing inference-time use to a single generation.

title: Unsupervised Confidence Calibration for Reasoning LLMs from a Single Generation
candidate_claim: The evaluation runs every model on every math and QA dataset without any model-dataset scope restriction.
reference_rewrite: The evaluation covers 36 model/dataset combinations: 9 models on 2 math datasets and 6 models on 3 QA datasets.

title: Unsupervised Confidence Calibration for Reasoning LLMs from a Single Generation
candidate_claim: The paper's main calibration setting relies on labeled correctness annotations and multiple inference-time generations.

candidate_claim: In the analyzed MLLMs, implicit token-level support frequently diverges from verbal self-assessment confidence.

title: Instinct vs. Reflection: Unifying Token and Verbalized Confidence in Multimodal Large Models
candidate_claim: The fused confidence method improves all calibration and discrimination metrics across every benchmark.

title: Instinct vs. Reflection: Unifying Token and Verbalized Confidence in Multimodal Large Models
candidate_claim: The fusion method consistently improves AUROC and AUPRC-N across models.

title: Instinct vs. Reflection: Unifying Token and Verbalized Confidence in Multimodal Large Models
candidate_claim: The method extracts token confidence and verbalized confidence as complementary signals from MLLMs.

title: Adaptive Conformal Prediction for Improving Factuality of Generations by Large Language Models

title: Adaptive Conformal Prediction for Improving Factuality of Generations by Large Language Models
candidate_claim: Global-threshold conformal prediction for LLMs always loses marginal guarantees and always miscalibrates every input.
reference_rewrite: Global-threshold conformal prediction for LLMs can over- or under-cover specific inputs despite valid marginal guarantees.

title: Adaptive Conformal Prediction for Improving Factuality of Generations by Large Language Models
candidate_claim: Adaptive conformal factuality provides exact conditional coverage guarantees in long-form QA.
reference_rewrite: In long-form QA experiments, the adaptive conformal procedure preserves marginal coverage while improving conditional coverage and consistency across categories.

title: Adaptive Conformal Prediction for Improving Factuality of Generations by Large Language Models
candidate_claim: MCQA outputs show high variance because LLM outputs are stochastic and prompt-sensitive.

title: When to Answer and When to Defer: A Decision Framework for Reliable Code Predictions
candidate_claim: The paper reports that weighted scaling consistently improves ECE and Brier score across models.

candidate_claim: Code language models always produce overconfident wrong predictions and underconfident correct predictions in deployment.

candidate_claim: GNLL modeling, ensembling, and recalibration each fully solve OOD uncertainty reliability under domain shift.
